reasoning: The US national deposit rate for 12-month CDs currently sits at 1.64% as of November 2025, continuing a steady downward trend from 1.76% in August 2025 and 1.84% a year prior. Financial institutions have been gradually lowering deposit rates following earlier Fed rate cuts and in anticipation of an easing monetary environment. A Fed hold merely stabilizes this downward glide, as banks gradually reduce trailing CD rates. Given that the resolution criteria require the rate to strictly increase, a flat or declining rate resolves to NO. For the December 2025 date (a 1-month horizon), a reversal is very unlikely, so I predict 14%. For the February 2026 date, slightly more uncertainty exists over potential Fed surprises or data anomalies (similar to the jump temporarily observed around August), so I assign an 18% probability.
batch_queries: Will the US national deposit rate for 12-month certificates of deposit (CDs) have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-11-09?
